text.title: Retry Logic, Streaming, and StructuredOutputService
text.summary: Error-injecting retry pattern, streaming JSON limitations, and the StructuredOutputService abstraction for production pipelines.

rule.statement: On ValidationError retry, include the error message in the next prompt — blind retries rarely improve results because the model repeats the same mistake without knowing what failed.
rule.rationale: The model does not know it failed validation unless told. Adding the Pydantic error message to the prompt ("The previous response failed validation: {error}. Please fix: ...") guides it to correct the specific field.

rule.statement: Log all ValidationError instances with the raw LLM output — patterns in failures reveal schema improvement opportunities.
rule.rationale: Repeated failures on the same field indicate the schema description is inadequate or the field is genuinely ambiguous. Logs are the only way to identify and fix these patterns.

rule.statement: Do not parse partial JSON chunks from a streaming structured output call — partial JSON is not valid JSON and json.loads() will fail on every chunk except the last.
rule.rationale: Streaming + JSON mode produces one valid JSON only at completion. Attempting to parse intermediary chunks wastes compute and produces only errors. Stream for UX progress indicators only, not for parsing.

rule.statement: Centralize structured extraction in a StructuredOutputService that handles retries, schema injection, and validation; subagents receive typed Pydantic objects as inputs and outputs, not raw strings.
rule.rationale: Duplicating extraction logic across subagents creates inconsistent error handling and retry behavior. A shared service ensures uniform behavior across the pipeline.
